title: Duron Qel-Droma
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:Duronhead.jpg]] |name=Duron Qel-Droma |homeworld=[[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' |birth= |death=[[3993 BBY]], [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]{{Tales|23|Shadows and Light}} |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Brown]] |feathers= |eyes=Brown |skin=Light |cyber= |families=[[Qel-Droma family]] |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|The Force… works in strange ways.|Duron Qel-Droma|}} '''Duron Qel-Droma''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] from [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], most well-known for his exploits during the [[Great Hunt (Jedi)|Great Hunt]] in [[3993 BBY]]. A member of the [[Qel-Droma family]] of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], Qel-Droma was the cousin of Jedi Knight [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], who famously fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and became a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith]], battling the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]]. In the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] immediately after the conflict, it fell to Duron to restore his family name. In the aftermath of the war, [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]-created [[Sithspawn/Legends|monstrosities]] were spread out throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and still posed a threat. In response, the Jedi Order initiated the Great Hunt, intending to wipe out the beasts for good. Qel-Droma took up arms with a group of fellow Jedi, including fellow Human [[Shaela Nuur]] and the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Guun Han Saresh]]. Qel-Droma and his compatriots battled creatures such as [[Sith wyrm]]s and [[silan]], but their greatest enemy would be the [[Terentatek/Legends|terentatek]]—a massive, vicious beast that fed on [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Blood/Legends|blood]]. Qel-Droma and company trekked the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] in search of these monsters, clearing them off of [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] such as [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and earning a reputation as the Jedi Order's greatest [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]]. During the Great Hunt, Qel-Droma and Nuur developed romantic feelings for each other that stretched the limits of the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]]. Although this threatened to cause a rift in the group, as Saresh deeply disapproved, by 3993 BBY the Hunt was called to an end. However, Qel-Droma, Nuur and Saresh were sent on a secret [[Cleansing of Korriban|mission]] to clear the Sith [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], a planet deeply steeped in the dark side. As they were the Order's best hunters, the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] believed that the three could be successful, and would be able to rely on each other to resist the dark side's lure. However, once on Korriban, Qel-Droma and the others ran into severe difficulties. In order to obtain necessary information, Qel-Droma and Nuur were forced to perform illegal actions that went against Qel-Droma's moral code, and Qel-Droma began to be afflicted with a series of debilitating, prophetic [[Force vision/Legends|visions]]. On Korriban, Qel-Droma and Nuur gave into their passion, leading to a disgusted Saresh leaving the group. Without Saresh, Qel-Droma and Nuur set off together to the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]], eventually coming across a terentatek in a remote [[Shyrack cave|cave]]. However, the beast proved too much—Qel-Droma was mortally wounded while desperately trying to defend Nuur. Before he [[Death/Legends|died]], Qel-Droma foresaw that sometime in the future, a great Jedi named [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] would find his body, and use Qel-Droma's [[Qel-Droma robe|robe]] in a battle with the dark side. Nearly [[3956 BBY|thirty-seven years later]], this would come to pass, as Revan found Qel-Droma's remains before [[Battle of Rakata Prime|defeating]] the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Darth Malak]]. ==Biography== ===Shouldering the burden=== {{Quote|Ulic was the only Qel-Droma who ever fell to the dark side, Vrook. We cannot judge Duron—or any Jedi—by their blood alone.|Jedi Master [[Bala Nisi]] to fellow Dantooine Jedi Enclave Councilperson [[Vrook Lamar]]|}} [[File:DuronMakesContact.jpg|thumb|left|145px|Duron Qel-Droma during battle with a terentatek]] A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Duron Qel-Droma grew up on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. In his youth, Qel-Droma was close to his two cousins, brothers [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic]] and [[Cay Qel-Droma]]. Following in the footsteps of Ulic and Cay's [[Parent/Legends|mother]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Lien-Tsai Qel-Droma]],''[[Jedi Academy Training Manual]]'' all three were eventually discovered to be [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] and joined the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. In [[3996 BBY]], tragedy struck the [[Qel-Droma family]], as Ulic Qel-Droma fell to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Leading a [[Brotherhood of the Sith|Sith force]] against the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] in what was known as the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], Ulic [[Battle of Ossus (Great Sith War)|slew]] his brother Cay in [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|single combat]] on the [[Great Jedi Library/Legends|Jedi library]] world of [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. Although Ulic repented and abandoned his cause, he was [[Wall of light|stripped]] of his connection to [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] and put into self-imposed [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exile]]. As such, it became Duron Qel-Droma's responsibility to clear the family name, and over the ensuing [[Standard year/Legends|years]], he committed himself to doing this. Shouldering the burden, Qel-Droma battled [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] remnants in the [[Cleansing of the Nine Houses]], but the endeavors that Qel-Droma was most remembered for came in a Jedi campaign known as the [[Great Hunt (Jedi)|Great Hunt]]. Qel-Droma was one of a number of [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] who traveled [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] eradicating Sith [[Sithspawn/Legends|monstrosities]] such as [[Sith wyrm]]s, [[silan]], and [[Terentatek/Legends|terentateks]]. The terentatek was an [[Sith alchemy/Legends|alchemical]] creation of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith]], [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], and would prove to be Qel-Droma's greatest foe. Half the size of a [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]] beast, terentateks fed on [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Blood/Legends|blood]], making them a serious threat to Jedi.''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' After the end of the war, the Jedi Order began the Great Hunt, which intended to wipe out the terentateks across the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. Qel-Droma joined a Jedi party including the Humans [[Shaela Nuur]] and [[Cale Berkona]], and the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Guun Han Saresh]], and went headlong into the Hunt. Qel-Droma and Nuur went on to develop romantic feelings for each other that stretched the limits of the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]]. Qel-Droma proved to be an indispensable member of the group, using his mental Force [[Force power/Legends|powers]] to [[Mind trick/Legends|confuse]] the beasts while the other Jedi attacked. During the Hunt, Qel-Droma wore a [[Clothing/Legends|garment]] known as the [[Qel-Droma robe]], a family heirloom which originally belonged to his cousin Cay.''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' The Great Hunt was quite successful, clearing the dark side monsters from worlds such as [[Onderon/Legends|Onderon]], [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], and [[Tython/Legends|Tython]]. In [[3993 BBY]], with the Hunt winding down, Qel-Droma and his compatriots traveled to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], hoping to [[Cleansing of Tatooine|cleanse]] that world of terentateks. After putting down several terentateks, the group tracked the last such beast on Tatooine to a [[Cave/Legends|cave]], only to watch as the terentatek [[Death/Legends|killed]] [[Unidentified Jedi (Great Hunt)|one of their number]]. After Qel-Droma made contact with the terentatek's mind and distracted it, the terentatek was finally slain. Uninjured by the fighting, Qel-Droma put the dead monster on a [[Fire/Legends|burning pyre]] with the others, and used the Force to [[Telekinesis/Legends|carry]] his dead teammate back to their [[Starship/Legends|ship]]. ===Heart of darkness=== {{Quote|We did it, Shaela. The future… is full of light…|Duron Qel-Droma's dying words|}} Five [[Standard day/Legends|days]] later, the Great Hunt was officially called to an end. However, the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] had different plans—the Sith [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], a planet deeply steeped in the dark side, still needed to be cleansed. Needing someone to get the task done quickly and quietly, the High Council selected Qel-Droma, Nuur and Saresh for the job. Their rationale was twofold—firstly, the Council believed that Qel-Droma, Nuur and Saresh could slay any terentatek they encountered, and secondly, the Council thought that those three would be able to support each other and together avoid Korriban's dark temptations. The Jedi High Council delegated the responsibility of informing the group to the lesser [[Dantooine Jedi Enclave Council|Jedi Council]] at the [[Jedi Enclave]] on [[Dantooine/Legends|Dantooine]], who called the three before them two days after the supposed end of the Great Hunt. Although the group, especially Qel-Droma, was surprised by the announcement, they resolved to move forward. They shipped out to Korriban the next day, making their way to the well-populated [[Dreshdae]] settlement. Once there, they began their search for information. Their investigation led them to a [[Unidentified Dreshdae cantina|cantina]], where Qel-Droma and Nuur met with an [[Unidentified Ithorian cantina patron|Ithorian]] and a [[Unidentified Quarren cantina patron|Quarren]] who claimed that they would introduce them to a [[Unidentified half breed Massassi|half breed Massassi]] with knowledge of the planet's terentateks in exchange for a [[Kilogram/Legends|kilo]] of [[Spice/Legends|spice]]. These kinds of endeavors did not sit well with the idealistic Qel-Droma—after only a [[Standard week/Legends|week]], he was concerned that Korriban was changing the three Jedi for the worse. Qel-Droma had grown tired of killing terentateks, especially after his mental links with the creatures during his battles with them revealed to him that they had spirits and thoughts of their own. In a discussion with Nuur in their safehouse one night, he expressed these concerns, only to be interrupted by a strange and traumatic [[Force vision/Legends|vision]]—he had had several such experiences recently, and they were growing progressively worse. Qel-Droma saw visions of a [[Jedi Civil War|war yet to come]], with the [[Destruction of Taris|fiery destruction]] of a [[Taris/Legends|large cityscape]], a [[Bastila Shan|captured Jedi]] and a [[Darth Malak|disfigured Sith]]. After Nuur attempted to comfort him, the two succumbed to their feelings for each other and passionately kissed. At that moment, Saresh, who disapproved of their relationship, walked into the room and took the opportunity to lecture them on the [[Jedi Code/Legends|Jedi Code]]. A heated argument ensued between Nuur and Saresh, although Qel-Droma intervened. Saresh posed one final question to Qel-Droma and Nuur, about whether the two would be able to control their feelings for each other, and Qel-Droma answered that if it meant renouncing their love, they could not. Disappointed in his teammates, Saresh quit the group, telling them that their relationship put the mission in jeopardy. Before he left, Saresh gave Qel-Droma and Nuur a [[Keycard/Legends|keycard]] taken from a [[Unidentified Twi'lek Sith (Korriban)|Sith apprentice]] he seduced, that would grant them passage to the [[Valley of the Dark Lords/Legends|Valley of the Dark Lords]] where the terentateks would be found. Before Qel-Droma and Nuur departed for the Valley, Qel-Droma wrote an entry in his [[Datapad/Legends|datapad]] which detailed his feelings for Nuur and his disappointment with Saresh. [[File:ShaelaDurondead.jpg|thumb|right|210px|Duron Qel-Droma lies dead in Shaela Nuur's arms.]] Three days later, as they entered the Valley of the Dark Lords, Qel-Droma and Nuur felt through the Force that Saresh had died—he had gone to the [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] world of [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]] in order to [[Cleansing of Kashyyyk|slay]] a terentatek he had learned was there, only to fall prey to the beast. Qel-Droma met the knowledge of his friend's death with sadness, despite their previous disputes. Nevertheless, they trudged onward. Their search led them to a [[Shyrack cave|cave]] filled with many lesser beasts including [[Shyrack/Legends|shyracks]], and after exploring deeper they found their terentatek. As Nuur jumped into battle, Qel-Droma realized to his horror that he could not make contact with the terentatek's mind as he usually did. At that moment, the terentatek tossed Nuur aside. Desperately trying to defend his love, Qel-Droma sprinted forward and attacked the terentatek with his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], wounding the beast. However, the terentatek soon grabbed Qel-Droma with one of its enormous claws, mortally injuring him. As Qel-Droma lay dying with Nuur by his side, he had one final vision—sometime in the future, a Jedi named [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] would happen upon his remains and take the Qel-Droma [[Robe/Legends|robe]] for himself. This Jedi would wear the robe as he defeated the disfigured Sith from Qel-Droma's previous vision. In his last moments, this gave Qel-Droma peace, knowing that the [[Light side of the Force/Legends|light]] would prevail in the end. As such, Qel-Droma instructed Nuur to leave his body where it lay, so that Revan might find it as he had seen. With his last breaths, Qel-Droma pledged his love to Nuur, before dying. ===Legacy=== {{Quote|The Council felt it was too dangerous to send anyone else in search of their remains, so their exact fate is not known. But let their deaths serve as a warning to you, Padawan. The terentatek are an abomination, they are ruthless engines of death and destruction. The Council had no choice but to destroy them, for the greater good of the galaxy. There is not much I can tell you other than their names: Duron Qel-Droma, Guun Han Saresh and Shaela Nuur. They disappeared nearly forty years ago, long before my time at the Academy.|Deesra Luur Jada to Revan, years after Qel-Droma's death|}} [[File:ShadowsandLight2.jpg|thumb|left|150px|Revan was victorious over Malak]] Shattered by her love's death, Shaela Nuur resolved to continue the fight in the name of vengeance for Qel-Droma. Nuur tracked the wounded beast to the ancient [[Tomb of Naga Sadow|tomb]] of former Sith [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]]. There, she was set upon by a larger, healthy terentatek, and Nuur fell in combat. For many years, the exact fate of Qel-Droma, Nuur and Saresh was not known, although it seemed quite certain the three were dead. The Jedi Council figured it too dangerous to send anyone in search of their remains, so the [[Bone/Legends|bones]] of Duron Qel-Droma lay undisturbed for nearly thirty-seven years. However, their stories were not forgotten. Around [[3956 BBY]], Revan, the Jedi whom Qel-Droma had seen in his dying vision, was told of the saga of Qel-Droma, Nuur and Saresh by a Knight named [[Deesra Luur Jada]] on Dantooine. Revan soon embarked on a journey across the galaxy to find the ancient [[Rakata/Legends|Rakatan]] [[Star Map]]s, and his search eventually led to Korriban. As Qel-Droma had foreseen decades earlier, Revan happened upon his [[Skeleton/Legends|skeletal]] remains, finding the Qel-Droma robe and Qel-Droma's datapad. The final part of Qel-Droma's vision came true not long after, as Revan [[Battle of Rakata Prime|defeated]] the disfigured Sith, [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] [[Darth Malak|Malak]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|I'm asking if you can control your passion. Can you?''"
"''If it means renouncing our love, then no, Guun Han… we can't.|Guun Han Saresh and Duron Qel-Droma, regarding Qel-Droma's relationship with Shaela Nuur|}} [[File:DuronShaelaDantooine.jpg|thumb|250px|Duron Qel-Droma with Shaela Nuur on Dantooine]] Duron Qel-Droma was an idealistic young Jedi Knight, committed to the light. As such, Qel-Droma was constantly nervous while on the Sith world of Korriban of falling to the dark side, and was more conscious of the effect of the planet than his compatriots Nuur and Saresh. Although willing to do what it took to destroy the terentateks, his ethical code made it difficult for him to perform illegal acts such as running spice, which he was forced to do on Korriban. Qel-Droma's morals extended even to his prey, the terentateks. Although connecting to their minds reaffirmed Qel-Droma's belief in their evil nature, the fact that they thought at all gave him pause, and by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the mission to Korriban he was reluctant to kill the beasts. After Qel-Droma's cousin Ulic fell to the dark side, Qel-Droma was determined to restore the integrity of his family name, driving him to accomplish much of what he did. Qel-Droma was a light-skinned man with [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and brown [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. By 3993 BBY, Qel-Droma had fallen in love with fellow Jedi Shaela Nuur, feelings that were reciprocated, although not overtly expressed until their time on Korriban. Towards the end of the Great Hunt, Qel-Droma began to have debilitating, prophetic visions, which greatly frightened him—the Jedi found that Nuur was able to soothe his pain. On Korriban, Qel-Droma and Nuur finally gave into their feelings, leading to the fracturing of their group. Qel-Droma was ready to do anything to defend Nuur, including a reckless and foolhardy charge against a terentatek on Korriban, an action that led to his death. Although Qel-Droma and Guun Han Saresh were close friends, Qel-Droma refused to heed the Twi'lek's advice and stayed committed to his love for Nuur. However, Qel-Droma did not hold a grudge against Saresh, although he privately was frustrated with Saresh's disapproval of his relationship with Nuur and thought that Saresh's departure may have been for the best. Qel-Droma was still quite confident that he and Nuur would be able to slay a terentatek without Saresh's help. Unlike Nuur, Qel-Droma greeted the knowledge of Saresh's death with sadness, acknowledging that he and Nuur were as much responsible for the downfall of their group as Saresh. Although Qel-Droma's visions were originally traumatic, his final one conveyed to him a message of hope that allowed him to die in peace, happy that the future held a victory for the light. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|After all, Duron, Shaela and Guun Han are our greatest hunters…|Jedi Master [[Aleco Stusea]]|}} By 3993 BBY, Duron Qel-Droma was considered one of the best [[Hunting/Legends|hunters]] in the Jedi Order. However, unlike the other members of his group, Qel-Droma did not usually employ a lightsaber while fighting a terentatek, though he did employ a green-colored one while fighting other, lesser creatures, such as shyrack. Instead, Qel-Droma used his mental Force abilities to reach into the minds of his prey, confusing the beasts and making them vulnerable to attacks from the other Jedi accompanying him—this also allowed Qel-Droma to read the thoughts of the terentateks, giving him a greater understanding of the beasts. In his final battle on Korriban, however, Qel-Droma failed to make mental contact with the beast, forcing him to fight the terentatek with his lightsaber. Qel-Droma was also adept in using telekinesis, levitating the body of one fallen Jedi some distance after a battle with a terentatek, and at one point using a Force burst to shatter the [[Window/Legends|windows]] of a room. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Duronrobes.jpg|thumb|right|130px|A sketch of Duron Qel-Droma from Dustin Weaver's portfolio]] The character of Duron Qel-Droma was first introduced in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''. In the game, Qel-Droma appears only as a skeleton in the Shyrack cave of Korriban, which can be found by the player character, Revan. On Qel-Droma's body, the player can find the Qel-Droma robe and Qel-Droma's datapad. Qel-Droma's backstory was greatly expanded upon in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] "[[Shadows and Light]]," one of the stories featured in [[2005]]'s [[Star Wars Tales 23|''Star Wars Tales'' 23]]. The story serves as a sort of prequel to ''Knights of the Old Republic'', telling the story of how Qel-Droma ended up as a corpse on Korriban. In the comic, Qel-Droma was illustrated by [[Dustin Weaver]]. Several other mentions of Qel-Droma have been made in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[canon]]. Qel-Droma was mentioned in a Codex entry in the MMORPG ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'',''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' to go along with mentions in the reference book ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' and the ''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]''. ''Knights of the Old Republic'' also features the datapads of Shaela Nuur and Guun Han Saresh, and on several occasions they misspell Qel-Droma's first name as "Duran". In ''Shadows and Light'', Qel-Droma's vision depicts Revan as wearing the Qel-Droma robe when he defeats the game's villain, Darth Malak—however, the robe does not need to be worn by the player, or taken at all. ==Appearances== *{{Tales|23|Shadows and Light}} *''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' {{1st}} {{C|Appears as a skeleton}} *''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' {{C|Mentioned in Codex entry}} ==Sources== *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[Knights of the Old Republic Campaign Guide]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Duron Qel-Droma}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Duron Qel-Droma |fr=Duron Qel-Droma |pl=Duron Qel-Droma |pt=Duron Qel-Droma |deGA=1 }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Qel-Droma, Duron}} [[Category:Alderaanians]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Pre-Ruusan Jedi Knights]] [[Category:Qel-Droma family]]
